title: artemisia borealis subsp. wormskioldii
intro: Artemisia borealis subsp. wormskioldii, also known as Wormskjold's sage or Arctic mugwort, is a subspecies of the Arctic wormwood (Artemisia borealis) plant, native to the Arctic regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic qualities, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Artemisia borealis subsp. wormskioldii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
